Multi-Layer Variant Effect Report

Analysis Request

Validate rs12740374 (the classic SORT1 LDL-cholesterol causal variant) by scoring it with three independent deep-learning oracles: ChromBPNet for chromatin accessibility, LegNet for MPRA promoter activity, and AlphaGenome as a generalist model covering ChIP, histones and CAGE. A new user should be able to see at a glance whether the three oracles agree on direction, and which assay/cell type drove each call.
  • Tool: analyze_variant_multilayer
  • Oracle: legnet
  • Normalizer: chorus per-track v1
  • Tracks requested: 1 tracks
  • Generated: 2026-05-08 14:12 UTC

Variant: chr1:109274968 G>T

Oracle: legnet

Gene: SORT1

Other nearby genes: PSRC1, CELSR2, MYBPHL, SARS1

Summary: No strong regulatory effects detected across any layer.

How to read this report. Every numeric effect below is produced by a deep-learning model. The scale depends on the regulatory layer — see the formulas below so you can interpret +0.3 correctly.

Per-layer effect
Unit depends on the layer:
  • Promoter activity (MPRA) Δ (alt−ref) — alt − ref. Raw difference in predicted value (not a ratio). Useful when ref is near zero.

Interactive Genome Browser

Ref signal in grey, alt signal in colour. Red stripe marks the variant. Zoom in/out and pan to explore. Gene track (RefSeq) loaded automatically.

Signal rescaled using each track's genome-wide noise floor (p95) and peak threshold (p99): 0 = noise floor, 1.0 = top 1% of bins genome-wide. Peak shape preserved; tracks comparable across cell types.
